2606.30481.title: Situation Perception: A Necessary Primitive to Artificial Superintelligence
2606.30481.paper_summary: Current large language models are extraordinary statistical engines. They compress vast amounts of text into useful patterns and can explain science, write code, imitate reasoning, and participate in philosophical conversation. Yet pattern mastery is not the same as general intelligence. A human infant begins with little explicit knowledge, but gradually discovers object permanence, cause and effect, other minds, bodily agency, and the persistence of the physical world. We make an argument that the path to artificial superintelligence (ASI) depends on a missing capacity we call \emph{situation perception}: the ability to construct, revise, and act within internal simulations of possible worlds across latent time. \emph{ perception} requires at least three core components: abstract prediction, long-term compressed memory, and active learning guided by objectives. In this work, we analyse why modern large language models remain incomplete, and propose the appropriate tests for measuring progress and consequences of machines that can simulate futures, pursue self-directed goals, and possibly judge their own creators.
2606.30481.paper_summary_zh: 目前的大型語言模型是非凡的統計引擎。<lb/>它們將大量文本壓縮成有用的模式，並能解釋科學、編寫代碼、模仿推理，並參與哲學對話。<lb/>然而，模式掌握並不等同於一般智慧。<lb/>人類嬰兒起初擁有很少的明確知識，但逐漸發現物體恆存、因果關係、他人心智、身體能動性以及物理世界的持續性。<lb/>我們提出一個論點，認為通往人工超智能（ASI）的道路依賴於一種缺失的能力，我們稱之為\emph{情境感知}：在潛在時間內構建、修訂和行動於可能世界的內部模擬的能力。<lb/>\emph{感知}至少需要三個核心組件：抽象預測、長期壓縮記憶，以及由目標引導的主動學習。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們分析了為什麼現代大型語言模型仍然不完整，並提出了測量進展和機器能夠模擬未來、追求自我導向目標，並可能評判其自身創造者的後果的適當測試。
2606.30481.author: Ziqin Yuan et.al.
